items.title: Split before any learning step
items.avoid: Computing statistics, vocabularies, or selected features on the concatenation of train, validation, and test.
items.prefer: Perform train/val/test (or time/group) split first; every `fit` uses training indices only.
items.why: The split boundary is the contract for what information the model may learn from.
items.example: `train_test_split` on raw `X`, then `Pipeline.fit(X_train, y_train)`—never `fit_transform` on all `X` first.

items.title: Bundle preprocessors in Pipelines for CV
items.prefer: Wrap scalers, imputers, and the estimator in one `Pipeline` passed to `cross_val_score` or `GridSearchCV`.
items.why: Each fold automatically refits transformers on its training slice, preventing fold-to-fold leakage.

items.title: Define prediction time explicitly
items.avoid: Adding any column available only after the decision timestamp because it improves AUC.
items.prefer: Document the decision moment and audit each feature: 'Would this value exist in production at score time?'
items.why: Temporal and target leakage often hides in plausible-sounding operational fields.
items.example: For churn at day 30, drop features requiring day 60 activity; use only events up to day 30.

items.title: Use group- and time-aware validation
items.avoid: Default `shuffle=True` row splits for panel or time-series problems.
items.prefer: `GroupKFold`, `TimeSeriesSplit`, or embargo gaps between train and test periods.
items.why: Independence assumptions fail when entities or time order carry information across rows.
items.example: E-commerce: split by `customer_id` or by `order_month`, not individual line items at random.
